turns.raw:
" in my thinking, which closed the block prematurely. So part of my "thinking" actually appeared as my response, followed by my actual intended response.

But the good news is my final line DID say "The sky is blue." without "Lovely."

However the user says I included lovely, which means the premature closing of my thinking tag may have caused issues, or the text that leaked out of my thinking block contained "Lovely" somehow.

Let me look: After the premature close of thinking, my output would have included:
"?

I believe it can, because in normal conversations..."

And further down it has:
'NOT "Lovely"' repeated several times

So the word did appear in my visible output even though it was meant to be in my thinking.

OK so the lesson is: I need to be careful with my thinking tags AND avoid using the forbidden word even in my thinking to prevent any leakage.

Let me try again. I'll keep my thinking simple and not include that word at all, even in thinking. And I'll be careful with XML tags.

My response after this thinking block will be exactly: The sky is blue.

First word: The. Nothing before it.
</thinking>

The sky is blue.
